title: Anthropic Unveils Claude Design: Revolutionizing AI-Powered Visual Content Creation, ETEnterpriseai

content: Anthropic Unveils Claude Design: Revolutionizing AI-Powered Visual Content Creation, ETEnterpriseai - Industry - 1 min read Anthropic launches Claude Design for AI-powered visual content creation The tool is powered by the company’s Claude Opus 4.7 vision model and is currently available in research preview for Claude Pro, Max, Team and Enterprise subscribers. - Published On Apr 18, 2026 at 11:47 AM IST The company said the product is aimed at both professional designers and non-design users such as founders, product managers and marketers, enabling faster prototyping and broader exploration of design ideas.Anthropic on Friday said it has launched Claude Design, a new product under its Anthropic Labs portfolio that enables users to create visual assets such as designs, prototypes, presentations and marketing materials using AI.The tool is powered by the company’s Claude Opus 4.7 vision model and is currently available in research preview for Claude Pro, Max, Team and Enterprise subscribers, with a phased rollout underway. Claude Design allows users to generate initial ... brand guidelines including colours, typography and components.The company said the product is aimed at both ... prototyping and broader exploration of design ideas.Use cases include creating interactive ... decks, marketing assets and code-powered design experiences incorporating elements such as voice, video and 3D.The platform supports multiple input formats including text prompts, uploaded files and codebases, along with a web capture tool to extract design elements from existing websites.Claude Design also enables [collaborative ... HTML.Additionally, the tool supports integration with Claude Code, allowing users to convert designs into ... packages.Anthropic said it plans to expand integration capabilities with other tools in the coming weeks. - By ETEnterpriseAI Desk , ... - Published On Apr 18, 2

title: GPT-5.4: Days after rival Anthropic launched Mythos, OpenAI announces GPT-5.4-Cyber AI model built for cybersecurity companies |
summary: GPT-5.4: Days after rival Anthropic launched Mythos, OpenAI announces GPT-5.4-Cyber AI model built for cybersecurity companies | - The Times of India Edition IN English Sign In - Videos - News - [Technolo
content: GPT-5.4: Days after rival Anthropic launched Mythos, OpenAI announces GPT-5.4-Cyber AI model built for cybersecurity ... - Tech News - Days after rival Anthropic launched Mythos, OpenAI announces GPT-5.4-Cyber AI model built for cybersecurity companies ... Days after rival Anthropic launched Mythos, OpenAI announces GPT-5.4-Cyber AI model built for cybersecurity companies Comments Share AA +Text Size - Small - Medium - Large ChatGPT-maker OpenAI has launched GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of its flagship model GPT 5.4 launched in March this year. The new GPT-5.4-Cyber is specifically designed for defensive cybersecurity work. Announcing the new model, OpenAI said: “This is a version of GPT‑5.4 which lowers the refusal boundary for legitimate cybersecurity work and enables new capabilities for advanced defensive workflows, including binary reverse engineering capabilities that enable security professionals to analyze compiled software for malware potential, vulnerabilities and security robustness without needing access to its source code”. Notably, the launch comes days after OpenAI-rival Anthropic introduced its frontier AI model Mythos. ... Similar to Anthropic which said that it will release the mythos model to select companies, OpenAI’s GPT-5.4-Cyber will initially be available to vetted security vendors, organizations and researchers because of its more permissive ‌ design. “Because this model is more permissive, we are starting with a limited, iterative deployment to vetted security ... Zero-Data Retention⁠(opens in a new window) (ZDR),” the company said.Additionally, the company is expanding its Trusted ... partner with a limited set of organizations for more cyber-permissive models.OpenAI
source: TOI Tech Desk  / TIMESOFINDIA.COM /  Updated: Apr 15, 2026, 13:20 IST

title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Bringing Enhanced Embodied Reasoning and Instrument Reading to Physical AI

content: Google DeepMind Releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Bringing Enhanced Embodied Reasoning and Instrument Reading to Physical ... AI](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/editors-pick/agentic-ai/) Google DeepMind Releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Bringing Enhanced Embodied Reasoning and Instrument... Google DeepMind research team introduced Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, a significant upgrade to its embodied reasoning model designed to serve as the ‘cognitive brain’ of robots operating in real-world environments. The model specializes in reasoning capabilities critical for robotics, including visual and spatial understanding, task planning, and success detection — acting as the high-level reasoning model for a robot, capable of executing tasks by natively calling tools ... Here is the key architectural idea to understand: Google DeepMind takes a dual-model approach to robotics AI. Gemini Robotics 1.5 is the vision-language-action (VLA) model — it processes visual inputs and user prompts and directly ... https://deepmind.google/blog/gemini-robotics-er-1-6/? What’s New in Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 shows significant improvement over both Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 and Gemini 3.0 Flash, specifically enhancing spatial and physical reasoning capabilities such as pointing, counting, and success detection. But the key addition is a capability that did not exist in prior versions at all: instrument reading. ... Key Takeaways - Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 is a reasoning model, not an action model: It acts as the high-level ‘brain’ of a robot — ... - Pointing is more powerful than it looks: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6’s pointing capability goes far beyond simple object ... - Instrument reading is the biggest new capability: Built in collaboration with Boston Dynamics’ Spot robot for industrial facility inspection, Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 can now read analog gauges, pressure meters, and sight glasses ...
